claims.text: Recommendations are scoped to the evaluation of LLM-based agents, covering sub-areas such as core capabilities (planning, tool use, self-reflection, memory), application-specific agent benchmarks (web, SWE, scientific, conversational), generalist agent benchmarks, and multi-turn conversation evaluation.
claims.verification_reason: The Abstract (ev_ddb084a0) and Introduction (ev_ceb02e5) of the evaluation survey explicitly enumerate core capabilities (planning, tool use, self-reflection, memory), application-specific benchmarks (web, SWE, scientific, conversational), generalist agents, and the multi-turn survey (ev_2f58e, ev_985210) covers multi-turn conversation evaluation. The full list aligns directly.

claims.text: Only the two evaluation surveys themselves are present in the user-visible corpus: "A Survey on Evaluation of LLM-based Agents" and "Evaluating LLM-based Agents for Multi-Turn Conversations: A Survey." The other works cited or discussed in these surveys (e.g., AgentBench, HAL, MTU-Bench, MMDU, API-Bank, BotChat, HUMOD, Gaia, MetaTool) appear only as references within those surveys and are not in the inventory as standalone items.
claims.verification_reason: Corpus identity searches confirm only the two surveys (corpus_observation_identity_1/2). The cited works (AgentBench, HAL, MetaTool, MTU-Bench, MMDU, API-Bank, BotChat, HUMOD, Gaia) appear only as cited references within the survey passages (ev_0fcd65f, ev_aead9018, ev_cf8f947b), not as standalone inventory items.

claims.text: The corpus of two evaluation-focused surveys is sufficient to ground a narrower recommendation set on the evaluation of LLM-based agents and multi-turn conversational agents, but it is not sufficient to ground a general "important papers on LLM-based agents" recommendation covering the broader literature (planning, tool use, memory, multi-agent systems, general agent architectures) outside the evaluation sub-area.
claims.verification_reason: ev_8eb726c4 explicitly states the surveys exclude works proposing new agent architectures without distinct evaluation methodology, confirming the corpus is bounded to evaluation. ev_3e731d62 and ev_97e56b2 confirm multi-turn evaluation coverage. ev_2f58e, ev_aead9018, ev_cf8f947b show broader planning/tool-use/memory content appears only as referenced works, not as standalone corpus items—supporting insufficiency for a general agents recommendation beyond the evaluation scope.
